mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over consecutive 2-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal produces a negative information coefficient over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce a positive mean 20-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during a late-cycle expansion indicate capacity constraints and weakening manufacturing demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in triggered late-cycle periods, or the mechanism yields a positive information coefficient during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5 percent annually forces central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of high-inflation triggered instances, or the signal shows no predictive power in a walk-forward validation framework.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates demonstrate labor market tightening that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to generate a positive mean 60-day forward return, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDP growth revisions following a negative economic shock confirm macroeconomic stabilization, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative or flat during post-shock recovery periods with positive GDP revisions, or the signal produces a negative information coefficient in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates falling below 0.5 percent provide maximum monetary accommodation that stimulates risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 50 percent of triggered instances, or the threshold signal yields a negative rank information coefficient over rolling 2-year windows.
